methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $575M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
VulcanForms is a late-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $575M+. The valuation framework draws on a sector universe of 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private), including peers such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage-to-next-stage advancement to the $575M total raised proxy. This yields an implied valuation of $690M. Peer-median triangulation could not be reliably applied; while 11 same-sector peers exist in robotics and manufacturing, fewer than three datapoints met the narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparability. A broader sector median would introduce undue noise given the sparse filtered set.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $586M (low) to $793M (high), with a median of $690M. This single-method output reflects meaningful structural uncertainty: the lack of tight peer comparables in the narrow tier limits cross-validation, and valuation hinges on the durability of the 1.2x late-stage multiplier for this specific sector. Sensitivity to changes in comparable company performance and to the stability of robotics sector funding multiples warrants monitoring as new data emerges.
